metadata.name: qcfractalcompute
metadata.summary: A distributed compute and database platform for quantum chemistry.

methods.name: qcfractalcompute.testing_helpers.MockTestingComputeManager._submit_tasks
documentation: We have an executor and everything running, but we short-circuit the actual compute

classes.name: qcfractalcompute.testing_helpers.QCATestingComputeThread

methods.name: qcfractalcompute.testing_helpers.QCATestingComputeThread._stop
documentation: Classmethod because finalizer can't handle bound methods
Runs a compute manager in a separate process

modules.name: qcfractalcompute.compress
documentation: Helpers for compressing data to send back to the server

functions.name: qcfractalcompute.compress._compress_common
documentation: Compresses outputs of an AtomicResult or OptimizationResult, storing them in extras

functions.name: qcfractalcompute.compress._compress_native_files
documentation: Compresses outputs and native files, storing them in extras

functions.name: qcfractalcompute.compress._compress_optimizationresult
documentation:
Compresses outputs inside an OptimizationResult, storing them in extras

Outputs for the AtomicResults stored in the trajectory will be stored in the extras for that AtomicResult

functions.name: qcfractalcompute.compress.compress_result
documentation:
Compress outputs and native files inside results, storing them in extras. Then compress the whole result

Outputs and native files are put into the database compressed, so no decompression is done
until someone requests them (and then decompression happens on the client)

The compressed outputs are stored in extras. For OptimizationResult, the outputs for the optimization
are stored in the extras field of the OptimizationResult, while the outputs for the trajectory
are stored in the extras field for the AtomicResults within the trajectory

modules.name: qcfractalcompute.compute_manager_cli
documentation: A command line interface to the qcfractal server.

functions.name: qcfractalcompute.run_scripts.geometric_nextchain.capture_all_output
documentation: Captures all output, including stdout, stderr, and logging

methods.name: qcfractalcompute.apps.app_manager.AppManager.all_program_info
documentation:
Returns a dictionary of all program information.

The dictionary is has keys of program name, and values of a list of available versions.

classes.name: qcfractalcompute.apps.models.AppTaskResult
documentation: A result that is returned from a parsl future

modules.name: qcfractalcompute
documentation: Compute worker and manager for QCArchive/QCFractal

functions.name: qcfractalcompute.generic_wrapper.wrap_generic_function
documentation:
Wraps a generic function call to return a GenericTaskResult (or FailedOperation)

This function handles capturing stdout/sterr and timing as well. This function takes
the origin task_info (basically TaskQueueORM as a dictionary) and runs the specified function

functions.name: qcfractalcompute.executors.build_executor
documentation: Build a Parsl executor from a given ExecutorConfig

classes.name: qcfractalcompute.config.PackageEnvironmentSettings
documentation:
Environments with installed packages that can be used to run calculations

The compute manager will query these environments to see what packages are installed, and
direct appropriate calculations to them.

classes.name: qcfractalcompute.config.FractalServerSettings
documentation:
Settings pertaining to the Fractal Server you wish to pull tasks from and push completed tasks to. Each manager
supports exactly 1 Fractal Server to be in communication with, and exactly 1 user on that Fractal Server. These
can be changed, but only once the Manager is shutdown and the settings changed. Multiple Managers however can be
started in parallel with each other, but must be done as separate calls to the CLI.

Caution: The password here is written in plain text, so it is up to the owner/writer of the configuration file
to ensure its security.

classes.name: qcfractalcompute.compute_manager.InterruptableScheduler
documentation: A scheduler that can be interrupted

classes.name: qcfractalcompute.compute_manager.ManagerStatistics
documentation: Manager statistics

documentation: Obtain the maximum number of tasks that can be run on an executor

methods.name: qcfractalcompute.compute_manager.ComputeManager.name
documentation: Returns this manager's full name.

methods.name: qcfractalcompute.compute_manager.ComputeManager.start
documentation:
Starts the manager

This will block until stop() is called

methods.name: qcfractalcompute.compute_manager.ComputeManager.stop
documentation: Interrupts a running worker, causing it to shut down

methods.name: qcfractalcompute.compute_manager.ComputeManager.heartbeat
documentation: Provides a heartbeat to the connected Server.

methods.name: qcfractalcompute.compute_manager.ComputeManager._submit_tasks
documentation: Submits tasks to the parsl queue to be run

methods.name: qcfractalcompute.compute_manager.ComputeManager._update_deferred_tasks
documentation: Attempt to post the previous payload failures

methods.name: qcfractalcompute.compute_manager.ComputeManager.update
documentation:
Examines the queue for completed tasks and adds successful completions to the database
while unsuccessful are logged for future inspection.

Parameters
----------
new_tasks
    Try to get new tasks from the server

methods.name: qcfractalcompute.compute_manager.ComputeManager.preprocess_new_tasks
documentation:
Any processing to do to the new tasks

To be overridden by a derived class. Sometimes used to save the results for testing

methods.name: qcfractalcompute.compute_manager.ComputeManager.postprocess_results
documentation:
Any processing to do to the results

To be overridden by a derived class. Sometimes used to save the results for testing

This object maintains a computational queue and watches for finished tasks for different
queue backends. Finished tasks are added to the database and removed from the queue.
